connector_workflow.output: CC-storage URNs/assetIds for every supplied raw clip, the speech audio, and all photo sets
connector_workflow.note: Ingest all family-supplied raw media into CC storage so later video/audio/photo tools can reference stable assetIds. Establishes the assetId namespace reused throughout. Headless-confirmed upload [C].

connector_workflow.output: per-clip scene/transcript summaries with emotional-highlight notes (which moments: cake-cutting, greeting guests, grandchildren) for each raw event clip
connector_workflow.note: Async-widget [A]: summarize each raw clip to locate the most emotional, usable moments before any cutting, so the agent can choose which assetIds/segments to seed the sizzle. Transcript/scene summary only (per A5) — no precise timecodes claimed; the actual cut is made by quick_cut in step 5.

connector_workflow.output: transcript + content summary of the noisy on-stage tribute speech (the spoken tribute lines incl. the Hindi/Gujarati blessing phrases)
connector_workflow.note: Async-widget [A]: transcribe the raw speech so the agent knows the narration wording and which lines to surface as on-screen captions. Its OUTPUT feeds caption authoring (step 10) and the credits sheet — it is NOT fed into media_enhance_speech (enhance denoises raw audio directly). Reroute per verdict fix #4.

connector_workflow.output: verified list of the stored photo assetIds grouped per chapter (childhood/college/career/family/guru-recent)
connector_workflow.note: Headless [C] CC-scope search: confirm and organize the chronological photo assetIds so the montage chapters map to the 8-section flow. State-tracking checkpoint: which assetId belongs to which chapter; reused by grade (7), layout (11), and credits (17).

connector_workflow.output: tribute_cinematic_sizzle.mp4 — auto-cut ~20–40s 1920×1080 highlight reel from the chosen raw-clip assetIds, seeded by step 2's highlight picks
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE async-widget [A]: feed the raw-clip assetIds + step-2 highlight choices (target_duration ~30s, prompt = warm emotional family-tribute highlights, no flashy cutting) to auto-cut the cinematic sizzle that seeds the montage. Output→input: step 2 picks, step 5 cuts.

connector_workflow.output: narration_clean.wav — denoised, de-reverbed, level-normalized version of the on-stage tribute speech
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE async-widget [A]: clean the noisy PA voice capture (speech only — legitimate per A5) into a broadcast-clean warm narration bed. Consumes ONLY the raw audio assetId from step 1 (verdict fix #4 — does NOT consume the step-3 transcript). Becomes the primary VO track.

connector_workflow.output: all chapter photos regraded with a single warm cinematic Lightroom preset so the decades-spanning prints feel unified
connector_workflow.note: Headless [C]: apply one cinematic/warm preset across every photo set so 1960s prints and 2020s digital frames share a premium, non-flashy grade. Unifies the slideshow visually before assembly. Real Lightroom-preset tool (A1).

connector_workflow.output: licensed royalty-free soft piano / light-orchestral / flute score track(s) with real Stock asset IDs, downloaded to CC storage
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE headless [C]: license a soft, uplifting, royalty-free music bed matching the per-section mood tags from the script. License BEFORE use (A4). The music is delivered as the licensed stem AS-IS — it is NOT run through media_enhance_speech (which is voice-only). Real Stock IDs captured here are reused in the credits sheet (step 17).

connector_workflow.output: an elegant Express title/lower-third template (templateURN) suitable for the 8 section titles + the closing thank-you
connector_workflow.note: Interactive-widget [W]: returns Express templates in a gallery; the user picks the tasteful premium typography template (not flashy). Selection required before fill_text. Feeds the title fills in step 10.

connector_workflow.output: 8 filled section-title cards + 1 closing thank-you card — TEXT ONLY filled into the user-picked template, layout preserved
connector_workflow.note: Interactive-widget [W], TEXT ONLY (A3): fill the user-selected template with the exact 8 section titles, the closing thank-you, and any blessing-line captions surfaced from the step-3 transcript. It places NO image — only text. The rendered card frames are exported headless in step 11.

connector_workflow.output: static 1920×1080 PNG frames: the 8 section-title cards + closing card (from step 10) and per-section composited photo-layout frames (graded photos placed at fixed positions) — NO motion, NO timing
connector_workflow.note: Headless [C] InDesign-class STATIC renderer only (verdict fix #3, A2): render the title cards and fixed-position composited photo frames at 1920×1080. All slow zoom/pan and per-section duration TIMING is NOT done here — it is realized in the local ffmpeg mux (step 14). Pure static page/frame export.

connector_workflow.output: inline previews/checks confirming the sizzle, cleaned VO, licensed score, and rendered frames are all valid assetIds in CC storage before assembly
connector_workflow.note: Headless [C] state-check: preview/verify every produced asset (sizzle, VO, score, static frames) is retrievable and correct before the local render. Replaces the old step-12 media_summarize loudness claim, which A5 says media_summarize cannot produce. No dB figures sourced here — loudness/ducking is measured and applied locally in step 13/14.

connector_workflow.output: loudness/duration report (ffmpeg ebur128/loudnorm + ffprobe) on the cleaned VO and the licensed score, and the computed −14 to −18 dB ducking gain for the music bed
connector_workflow.note: Local glue [L]: measure VO and music integrated loudness/duration with ffmpeg/ffprobe (the tool that can actually report dB — verdict fix #2) and compute the sidechain/ducking target so the score sits ~14–18 dB under the VO. Off-connector measurement step feeding the mux.

connector_workflow.output: tribute_master_stage_screen.mp4 — 1920×1080, 5–8 min, H.264 master: static frames animated with slow Ken-Burns zoom/pan + crossfades, sizzle inset, cleaned VO, score ducked per step 13
connector_workflow.note: Local glue [L]: ffmpeg assembly of the static frames (11) with slow zoom/pan + crossfade MOTION and per-section durations from the script (verdict fix #3 — timing lives here), the sizzle (5), the cleaned VO (6), and the licensed score ducked to the step-13 gain. Produces the timed 8-section master.

connector_workflow.output: tribute_share_16x9_under60s.mp4 — a ≤60s 1920×1080 highlight pulled from the master's strongest stretch
connector_workflow.note: Async-widget [A]: verdict fix #1 — video_resize CANNOT trim, so first auto-cut a ≤60s 16:9 clip from the master (target_duration ≤60s, warm-highlights prompt). This LENGTH change is done by quick_cut, not resize. Output feeds the same-length reframe in step 16.

connector_workflow.output: tribute_social_share_cut.mp4 — 1080×1920 vertical, subject-aware reframe of the already-≤60s clip from step 15 (same length, no trim)
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE async-widget [A], verdict fix #1: subject-aware reframe of the ≤60s clip (from step 15) from 16:9 to 9:16, keeping the honoree centered. video_resize is SAME-LENGTH (A5) — the ≤60s trimming already happened in step 15, so no trim is implied here.

connector_workflow.output: asset_credits_sheet (1-page A4 layout, PNG/PDF) listing every licensed Stock track (IDs from step 8), source assets (step 4), narration source (step 3), and final render specs from the step-14 master
connector_workflow.note: Headless [C], verdict-aware: build the 'music and asset details used' credits page from a genuine layout (A2 render, NOT document_convert_pdf which cannot merge/author pages). Pulls the real Stock license IDs (8), the photo/clip inventory (4), the narration source (3/6), and the delivered master's specs (14). Export step 18 finalizes the PDF.

connector_workflow.output: final delivery checkpoint: preview all five deliverables (master, vertical cut, sizzle, cleaned VO, credits sheet PDF) confirmed retrievable in CC storage with correct specs
connector_workflow.note: Headless [C]: final addressable-asset checkpoint so each intermediate (cleaned VO, sizzle, master, vertical cut, credits) stays individually retrievable for the client's promised 1–2 revision rounds. Closes the contract.

corrections_applied: VERTICAL-CUT ROUTING (verdict MAJOR / fix #1): split the old single video_resize step into TWO steps — step 15 video_create_quick_cut [A] does the 5-8min→≤60s LENGTH change, then step 16 video_resize [A] does the SAME-LENGTH 16:9→9:16 subject-aware reframe. Removed all 'trim to ≤60s as part of the resize pass' language since video_resize is same-length per A5.

DUCKING/LOUDNESS (verdict MINOR / fix #2): media_summarize can only return transcript/scene summaries (A5), not dB loudness. Removed the old step-12 'level/loudness report' from media_summarize. The −14 to −18 dB ducking is now MEASURED and computed in a local ffmpeg/ffprobe ebur128 step (new step 13) and applied in the local mux (step 14).

STATIC vs MOTION (verdict MINOR / fix #3): document_render_layout is an InDesign-class STATIC page renderer (A2), not a video EDL/motion engine. Reworded step 11 to render only static 1920×1080 title cards and fixed-position composited photo frames — and moved ALL slow zoom/pan + per-section duration TIMING into the local ffmpeg mux (step 14) where motion is genuinely realized.

WEAK CHAIN (verdict fix #4): rerouted step 3's media_summarize transcript so it feeds on-screen CAPTION/script authoring (step 10) and the credits sheet (step 17), and made media_enhance_speech (step 6) consume ONLY the raw audio assetId — removing the false 'transcript-as-input-to-enhance' dependency.

fill_text MISUSE GUARD: confirmed fill_text (step 10) places TEXT ONLY into the user-picked template — it never places an image. The rendered title-card FRAMES are exported by document_render_layout (step 11), and image placement/compositing of photos is done by document_render_layout (static) + the local ffmpeg mux, never by fill_text.

MUSIC MISUSE GUARD: confirmed media_enhance_speech is run ONLY on the noisy VOICE recording (legitimate audio-repair, A5). The licensed music stem is delivered AS-IS from asset_license_and_download_stock (step 8) and is never fed through media_enhance_speech (which is voice-only, not a music/loudness processor).

CREDITS PDF (document_convert_pdf misuse removed): the old step-15 used document_convert_pdf to assemble/merge a multi-source credits sheet — but document_convert_pdf is PDF→.indd and CANNOT merge/author pages (A2, A6). Replaced with document_render_layout (step 17) authoring a genuine 1-page credits layout, then asset_inline_preview (step 18) to finalize/verify the PDF deliverable.

EXEC-MODE TAGGING: tagged every step — asset/photo/static-layout/preview headless = C; Express text track (search_design, fill_text) = W; all video/audio connector steps (media_summarize, video_create_quick_cut, media_enhance_speech, video_resize) = A; local ffmpeg measure + mux = L. headless_doable set FALSE because the honest workflow includes W and A steps.

LONG-HORIZON PRESERVED: expanded from 15 to 18 ordered steps (16 connector calls + 2 local) across video/audio, stock/storage, photo, Express-template, and document-layout groups, keeping the genuine output→input chain and heavy asset reuse intact.

FRAME-COUNT/DURATION GAP: made the duration math explicit — master 5-8min (step 14), sizzle ~20-40s (step 5) reused inside the master, and the vertical share cut is now provably ≤60s because step 15 quick_cut produces a ≤60s source BEFORE the same-length reframe in step 16.

ORPHANED-INPUT FIX: every input.used_in_steps now references a step that truly consumes it — photos at [1,4,7,11]; raw_home_event_clips at [1,2,5,14]; onstage_tribute_speech_raw at [1,3,6]; narration_script_and_milestones at [8,9,10,11,17] (it genuinely drives the music query, template search, text fills, static layout, and credits).
difficulty_rationale: Hard on every long-horizon axis. (1) Horizon & breadth: 16 ordered connector calls + 2 local steps spanning five capability groups (video/audio, stock/storage, photo, Express template, document layout), with all must-exercise video+audio tools in play and a strict execution-mode discipline (C/W/A/L) the agent must respect — it cannot, e.g., expect headless retrieval of the [A] video/audio results or auto-pick the [W] Express template. (2) Heavy state tracking: a stable assetId namespace from step 1, remembering which photo assetId belongs to which of the 8 named chapters, which raw-clip segments the summaries flagged, and which Stock IDs were licensed — then surfacing those exact Stock IDs again 9 steps later in the credits sheet. (3) Asset reuse: nearly every input is consumed in 3-4 steps (photos: ingest→organize→grade→layout→credits; raw clips: summarize→cut→mux; speech: summarize-for-captions / enhance / credits). (4) Capability traps the agent must avoid: NOT trimming with video_resize (must quick_cut first), NOT sourcing dB loudness from media_summarize (must measure in ffmpeg), NOT treating document_render_layout as a motion/EDL engine, NOT running music through media_enhance_speech, NOT using document_convert_pdf to author the credits page. (5) Exact-spec pressure: a 1920×1080 master in a strict 5-8 min window, music ducked 14-18 dB under the VO, a separate exact 1080×1920 ≤60s vertical cut, and a credits PDF whose stated specs must match the delivered master. (6) Aesthetic judgment under a 'no-flashy' constraint: slow transitions and tasteful titles, not a punchy social sizzle. (7) True chaining: the master is only correct if the sizzle, cleaned narration, licensed score, and unified grade were all produced and threaded correctly upstream — one broken link is visible in the deliverable."
full_brief:
Context: A family is organizing a "Guru Vandana" (guru-reverence) cum birthday celebration for Shri Anil Tolia Ji, to be attended by roughly 250 friends, family members, children, and guests. The centerpiece of the function is an emotional life-journey tribute video projected on a large LED screen/projector during the event. The piece must feel emotional but positive, respectful and heartwarming, inspirational, elegant and premium — and must AVOID loud flashy editing, overly dramatic effects, fast social-media-style cutting, and distracting animations. The desired register is "cinematic family tribute / elegant slideshow-documentary," with smooth transitions, slow zoom/pan, and tasteful typography.

The ask: Using the supplied raw materials — chronological photo sets (childhood, college & friendship, career, marriage & family, recent, and Guru-Vandana moments), several raw home/event video clips, and a noisy live on-stage tribute speech recorded at rehearsal — produce a 5-8 minute Full-HD (1920×1080, MP4) tribute that follows the client's exact 8-section flow: (1) Opening Tribute, (2) Childhood Journey, (3) College & Friendship Days, (4) Career & Achievements, (5) Family Journey, (6) Guru Vandana Section, (7) Birthday Celebration, (8) Closing Message. The agent must mine the raw clips for the most emotional, usable moments, auto-cut a tight cinematic "memory sizzle" from the chosen clip segments to seed the montage, clean the noisy on-stage tribute speech into a warm, broadcast-clean narration track (or a warm/mature English VO with optional Hindi/Gujarati blessing lines), score the whole thing with soft, licensed, royalty-free piano/light-orchestral/flute music that blends under — and never overpowers — the narration, and lay elegant section titles and a closing thank-you over the cut.

Constraints & deliverables: Output 1 = the stage-screen MASTER at exactly 1920×1080 (16:9), H.264 MP4, 5-8 minutes, clean enough for large-screen projection, music bed mixed roughly 14-18 dB under the VO. Output 2 = a short (≤60s) vertical 1080×1920 (9:16) social/WhatsApp share cut derived from the master's best moments (subject-aware reframed), same audio treatment. Output 3 = the cinematic sizzle (intermediate, reused in the master, kept addressable). Output 4 = the cleaned narration WAV. Output 5 = an asset-credits / music-and-source sheet (PDF) listing every licensed Stock track and the source clips used, as the client explicitly requires "music and asset details used." All music must be royalty-free/licensed; narration must be warm, encouraging, emotional, mature and respectful; nothing flashy. The family expects one or two revision rounds, so the agent must keep the intermediate assets (cleaned VO, licensed score, the sizzle, the master) individually addressable for re-edits.

EXECUTION-MODE NOTE (per CONNECTOR_CAPABILITIES_v2.1): this brief is PRODUCT-doable, not autonomous-headless. The Express title track (search_design, fill_text) needs a user gallery pick = [W]; the video/audio connector tools (media_summarize, video_create_quick_cut, media_enhance_speech, video_resize) are async-widget = [A] and notify in the product; photo grading, asset search, static layout rendering, and inline preview are headless = [C]; loudness measurement and the final mux/trim are local ffmpeg = [L]. Capability discipline matters: video_resize is SAME-LENGTH (the ≤60s trim is done by video_create_quick_cut first), media_summarize gives transcripts/scene summaries only (no dB loudness — that is measured locally), document_render_layout renders only STATIC 1920×1080 frames (all motion/timing is in the ffmpeg mux), media_enhance_speech is run only on the VOICE (the licensed music stem is delivered as-is), and the credits page is authored with document_render_layout (document_convert_pdf cannot merge/author pages).
grounding_note:
REAL (verbatim from the client's fulldesc in db_all.json): an "Emotional Life Journey Tribute Video" for the "Guru Vandana" + Birthday celebration of Shri Anil Tolia Ji, to be "played on a large screen during the family function in front of friends, family members, children, and guests" (~250 people). The 8-section storytelling flow is real and quoted exactly: (1) Opening Tribute, (2) Childhood Journey, (3) College & Friendship Days, (4) Career & Achievements, (5) Family Journey, (6) Guru Vandana Section, (7) Birthday Celebration, (8) Closing Message. Real: warm/mature/respectful voice narration in primarily English with optional Hindi/Gujarati elements (AI narration explicitly allowed); soft piano / light orchestral / flute royalty-free background music that "blends naturally with visuals" and does "not overpower narration"; smooth cinematic transitions, slow zoom/pan, elegant typography; preferred duration 5-8 minutes; Full HD 1920×1080 MP4 "suitable for LED screen/projector playback"; deliverables = final HD MP4 + editable/source files + "music and asset details used" + one or two revision rounds; budget ₹1500-₹12500 INR; tools the client named include Premiere Pro, After Effects, Canva Pro, and AI narration (Synthesia). The "Content Available" list is real: childhood, college, friends/social, professional/work, marriage & family, recent, and Guru-Vandana photos, plus "Additional content may include short messages/quotes, milestones/dates, family inputs."

ENRICHED (only where the brief genuinely implies it, made explicit so it is honestly long-horizon): The brief says the tribute is built "through photographs, music, voice narration, and cinematic storytelling" and lists raw "family inputs" — I enrich the supplied content to include a handful of RAW EVENT/HOME VIDEO CLIPS (a noisy on-stage live tribute speech a family elder gives, plus candid home/career clips) alongside the photos, because a 250-guest live function realistically yields phone video and because the named toolset (Premiere/After Effects) is video-editing software. This makes media_summarize (find emotional highlights across raw clips), video_create_quick_cut (auto-cut a sizzle from chosen assetIds), and media_enhance_speech (clean the noisy live on-stage speech) all faithful to the deliverable rather than invented. The "stage-screen master + a short social/WhatsApp share cut" is a light, defensible enrichment: the family explicitly wants the piece for a large screen, and event families routinely also want a vertical clip to share afterward — the brief's audience ("friends, family members, children, and guests") supports this. Nothing about the emotional, non-flashy, premium, royalty-free, HD-MP4 contract is changed.

CAPABILITY-GROUNDING (v2.1): every step is execution-mode tagged and routed to a tool that can actually do it. The corrected chain removes the original feasibility errors flagged by the adversarial verdict (video_resize used to trim; media_summarize used to report dB; document_render_layout used as a motion/EDL engine; transcript falsely fed into enhance_speech) and the latent tool-misuse risks (document_convert_pdf used to author a multi-source page; music run through speech enhancement).

inputs.name: childhood_journey_photos
inputs.gen_prompt: A small set (treat as 6-8 frames) of authentic-looking 1960s-70s Indian family snapshots of a baby/young boy named Anil: faded warm-toned film photographs, slightly soft focus, real film grain and dust, period-correct clothing, a modest middle-class Gujarati home, a school class photo on a bench, a boy in shorts holding a slate. Genuine vintage photo-paper texture, real skin with natural imperfections, individual flyaway hairs, slightly off white balance as old prints have. Candid and unposed, documentary, NOT a glossy AI render, no modern artifacts, no text watermark.
inputs.realism_notes: Must read as scanned old prints: uneven exposure, paper grain, a fold crease or two, period color cast. No CGI sheen. Used as the chronological opening of the montage and to seed the section-1/2 mood.

inputs.name: college_friendship_photos
inputs.gen_prompt: Authentic late-1980s Indian college-life snapshots of the same man (Anil) as a young adult: friends grouped on hostel steps, a cricket-team photo, a scooter, a canteen moment. Mild 1980s film grain, warm faded color, real candid expressions, natural skin pores and stubble, individual hair strands and flyaways, correct hands. Documentary, slightly imperfect framing as friends' cameras would shoot. NOT glossy, no modern phones, no text overlay.
inputs.realism_notes: Period clothing/hairstyles and analog color shift sell the 'college & friendship days' chapter. Candid, not staged-perfect.

inputs.name: career_achievement_photos
inputs.gen_prompt: Professional/work-life photos of the same man across his 40s-50s: at an office desk, receiving a small award/momento on a stage, shaking hands at a business event, a factory or shop-floor visit. Realistic 2000s-2010s digital-camera look, natural indoor lighting with slight flash, real skin texture with pores and faint wrinkles, correct hands and fingers, lanyard/spectacles. Candid documentary, not a stock-photo smile, no text or logos baked in.
inputs.realism_notes: Should look like genuine event/work photos across two decades. Reused for the 'career & achievements' chapter and again, color-graded, in the closing recap.

inputs.name: marriage_family_photos
inputs.gen_prompt: Warm Indian marriage and family photographs of the same man: a 1990s wedding portrait in traditional Gujarati attire with garlands, later family photos with wife and growing children, a festival gathering, grandchildren in recent years. Mix of slightly-faded 90s prints and crisp recent digital frames. Real skin, individual hair strands, genuine candid emotion (a tear, a laugh), correct hands, authentic jewelry and clothing. Documentary family-album feel, NOT CGI-glossy, no watermark.
inputs.realism_notes: Emotional core of the piece. Spans decades so the grade must unify them. Reused across family + closing chapters.

inputs.name: guru_vandana_recent_photos
inputs.gen_prompt: Recent (2020s) photographs themed around guru-reverence and the honoree as an elder/mentor: the man touching an elder guru's feet in respect, garlanding a guru, sitting in a satsang/temple setting, mentoring younger people, a recent crisp portrait in a kurta with a warm dignified expression. Natural daylight and lamp-lit temple tones, real skin with age texture, individual grey hairs, correct hands in pranam gesture. Respectful, documentary, premium but authentic — NOT an AI-perfect render, no text.
inputs.realism_notes: Carries the 'Guru Vandana' chapter and the dignified closing portrait. Recent, sharp, but still candid.

inputs.name: raw_home_event_clips
inputs.gen_prompt: A handful of raw, lightly-shaky handheld phone clips (1080p, native audio) from family life and the run-up to the function: the honoree laughing with grandchildren in a living room; cutting a cake at a small home gathering with off-camera chatter; a slow pan across a decorated stage with marigold garlands before guests arrive; the honoree walking in and folding hands to greet people. Natural ambient room sound, imperfect handheld framing, real people with natural skin/hair, candid documentary look. NOT a polished commercial; phone-video realism with minor motion blur.
inputs.realism_notes: These are the raw clips the agent must SUMMARIZE to find emotional highlights and then QUICK-CUT into a sizzle, and re-reference in the local mux. Native-audio 1080p (not 720p veo-fast). Several distinct moments so summarization and selection are non-trivial.

inputs.name: onstage_tribute_speech_raw
inputs.gen_prompt: A ~90-second warm spoken tribute in English with a few Hindi/Gujarati blessing phrases, delivered as if by a family elder at a microphone: 'Tonight we honour Anil bhai — a son, a friend, a guide... aaj hum sab unke charno mein...'. Warm, mature, emotional, respectful male voice. Generate clean at 48 kHz, THEN (for this audio-repair task only) the supplied file is degraded to simulate a live PA capture: add room reverb, hum, mic pops, crowd murmur and uneven levels, so the agent has a genuinely noisy on-stage recording to enhance.
inputs.realism_notes: This is the ONE input that is intentionally roughened, because the task literally includes audio-repair: the agent must run media_enhance_speech (voice-only, legitimate) on it to recover a broadcast-clean narration bed. Content is the real tribute language the family would use. Summarized once (step 3) for caption content, enhanced once (step 6) for the VO.

inputs.name: narration_script_and_milestones
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a JSON file with: (a) the 8 section titles exactly per the client flow (Opening Tribute, Childhood Journey, College & Friendship Days, Career & Achievements, Family Journey, Guru Vandana Section, Birthday Celebration, Closing Message); (b) for each section, an elegant on-screen caption line and an estimated duration in seconds (totalling 5-8 min); (c) key milestones/dates and short quotes the family supplied; (d) a warm 6-8 line closing thank-you message; (e) a music-mood tag per section (soft piano / light orchestral / flute). Mature, respectful, English with two optional Hindi/Gujarati blessing lines.
inputs.realism_notes: Drives the typography, pacing, chapter structure, and the music-mood query. Reused when licensing music, finding/filling titles, laying static frames, and building the credits sheet.

iterative_chaining_note: The chain is genuinely output→input throughout, with capability-honest routing. (1) asset_add_file mints the assetId namespace every later tool references. (2/3) media_summarize [A] reads the raw clips and the noisy speech; (5) video_create_quick_cut [A] consumes step-2's highlight picks to auto-cut the sizzle; (6) media_enhance_speech [A] consumes ONLY the raw speech audio (step-1 assetId) to produce narration_clean.wav — the step-3 transcript instead feeds caption authoring (step 10) and credits (step 17), removing the false 'transcript-into-enhance' dependency the verdict flagged. (4→7) asset_search [C] organizes the photo assetIds, then image_apply_preset [C] regrades exactly those into a unified look. (8) asset_license_and_download_stock [C] returns real Stock IDs consumed twice — by the mux and by the credits page — and the music is delivered as-is (never run through speech enhancement). (9→10) search_design [W] template + the script JSON feed fill_text [W] to make the 8 TEXT-ONLY title cards. (11) document_render_layout [C] renders those cards + fixed-position composited photo frames as STATIC 1920×1080 PNGs (no motion). (12) asset_inline_preview [C] verifies every produced asset. (13) local ffmpeg [L] MEASURES VO/score loudness (the only place dB can come from) and computes the −14 to −18 dB ducking gain. (14) the local ffmpeg mux [L] animates the static frames with slow zoom/pan + crossfades (timing lives here), threads the sizzle (5), the VO (6), and the score ducked to the step-13 gain into the timed master. (15) video_create_quick_cut [A] trims a ≤60s 16:9 clip from the master (length change — the verdict's MAJOR fix), then (16) video_resize [A] reframes that already-≤60s clip 16:9→9:16 SAME-LENGTH. (17) document_render_layout [C] authors the credits page from the Stock IDs (8), asset inventory (4), narration source (3/6), and master specs (14) — replacing the misused document_convert_pdf. (18) asset_inline_preview [C] is the final addressable-deliverable checkpoint for the client's revision rounds. No step invents data it wasn't handed by an earlier step, and no step is asked to do something its tool cannot do.
one_line_ask: Assemble a 5-8 minute cinematic, emotionally-paced Full-HD 1920×1080 tribute video for Shri Anil Tolia Ji's Guru Vandana + birthday function — built from the family's raw photos and event clips, following the 8-section life-journey flow, with a cleaned warm narration VO over soft royalty-free piano/orchestral score — and deliver both the stage-screen master and a short vertical social share cut plus an asset-credits sheet.

outputs.name: tribute_master_stage_screen.mp4
outputs.spec: 1920×1080 (16:9), H.264 MP4, 5-8 minutes, ~24-30 fps, follows all 8 client sections in order, cleaned warm narration VO over a licensed soft piano/orchestral score mixed ~14-18 dB under the VO (ducking measured + applied locally), elegant static section titles + closing thank-you with slow zoom/pan + crossfades, smooth slow transitions only (no flashy cuts). Suitable for LED-screen/projector playback at the 250-guest function.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: exact 1920×1080; runtime within 5-8 min; all 8 named sections present and in order; narration is clean/intelligible (no PA hum/crowd from the raw speech survives); music audibly sits UNDER the VO (~14-18 dB) and is the soft/uplifting style requested (not flashy); pacing is slow/cinematic with no fast social-style cutting; titles are tasteful and match the script; emotional, premium, respectful tone.

outputs.name: tribute_social_share_cut.mp4
outputs.spec: 1080×1920 (9:16) vertical, H.264 MP4, ≤60 seconds, subject-aware reframed (same-length) from a ≤60s quick-cut of the master's strongest moments, same cleaned VO + score, suitable for family WhatsApp/Instagram share.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: exact 1080×1920 vertical; ≤60s (achieved via a quick_cut BEFORE the same-length resize, not by trimming inside resize); the honoree stays centered/in-frame after reframing (subject-aware, no decapitated heads); audio matches the master's clean mix; it is clearly derived from the master, not a separate edit.

outputs.name: tribute_cinematic_sizzle.mp4
outputs.spec: 1920×1080, H.264 MP4, ~20-40 seconds, the auto-cut highlight reel of the strongest raw-clip moments used to seed the montage (intermediate deliverable kept addressable for revisions).
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: the sizzle is genuinely built from the chosen raw-clip segments (traceable to the step-2 summarization picks), is tight and emotional, and is reused inside the master rather than discarded.

outputs.name: narration_clean.wav
outputs.spec: 48 kHz WAV (or AAC), the enhanced/denoised version of the on-stage tribute speech — broadcast-clean, PA hum/reverb/crowd removed, levels normalized.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator A/Bs against onstage_tribute_speech_raw: noise/reverb/crowd reduced, intelligibility and warmth preserved, no robotic artifacts, consistent loudness; produced by media_enhance_speech on the raw voice only (not a music processor).

outputs.name: asset_credits_sheet.pdf
outputs.spec: A4/Letter PDF (authored via document_render_layout, not a PDF-merge tool) listing every licensed Stock music track (title + Stock asset ID + license), each source clip/photo set used, the narration source, and final render specs — satisfying the client's 'music and asset details used' deliverable.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: every licensed track and source asset actually used in the master is listed with its real Stock ID; render specs (1920×1080, runtime) match the delivered master; readable, organized, complete.

title: Cinematic Tribute Video for Guru Vandana cum Birthday Event (Shri Anil Tolia Ji, 250 guests)

residual_issues: document_render_layout SCOPE OVERREACH (steps 11 & 17): A2 says it only EXPORTS a GENUINE pre-existing .indd/.idml to PDF/JPEG/PNG. It does NOT author a layout, composite graded photos at fixed positions, or ingest step data. Step 11 ('render per-section composited photo-layout frames, graded photos placed at fixed positions') and step 17 ('build the credits page from a genuine layout' out of Stock IDs/inventory) both assume authoring/compositing the tool cannot do. Verdict fix #3 fixed only the motion overclaim, not the authoring overclaim. Per the sheet (A3 note + section B), headless composition must go through CANVA or local PIL [L] -- these two steps are misrouted.

Express->InDesign-render misroute (steps 10->11): fill_text produces a filled ADOBE EXPRESS design, but document_render_layout exports .indd/.idml ONLY (A2). An Express design is not a .indd/.idml, so step 11 cannot export the fill_text title cards via document_render_layout. The Express title cards must be exported through the Express/Canva track, not an InDesign-class renderer.

Minor tagging nuance: search_design is labeled '[C-ish]' in A3 (returns templates headless) but the spec tags the step [W]. Defensible because fill_text downstream needs a user gallery pick and the EXECUTION MODES block lists search_design under [W]; not a defect, just note the sheet's dual labeling.
